prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_sampling_plan production_unit_1)
(assign_quality_reviewer_to_plan production_unit_1 quality_reviewer_1)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(confirm_assay_method_assignment production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(allocate_sampling_kit_to_production_point production_unit_1 production_sampling_point_a sampling_kit_a)
(complete_production_sampling_with_kit production_unit_1 production_sampling_point_a assay_method_a sampling_kit_a)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(initialize_sampling_plan qc_unit_1)
(assign_quality_reviewer_to_plan qc_unit_1 quality_reviewer_2)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan qc_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(confirm_assay_method_assignment qc_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(allocate_sampling_kit_to_qc_point qc_unit_1 qc_sampling_point_a sampling_kit_a)
(complete_qc_sampling_with_kit qc_unit_1 qc_sampling_point_a assay_method_a sampling_kit_a)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan qc_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(create_and_link_sample_container production_unit_1 qc_unit_1 production_sampling_point_a qc_sampling_point_a sample_container_001)
(check_in_container_to_lab sample_container_001 production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(initialize_sampling_plan production_batch_001)
(assign_quality_reviewer_to_plan production_batch_001 quality_reviewer_3)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan production_batch_001 assay_method_a)
(confirm_assay_method_assignment production_batch_001 assay_method_a)
(vialize_sample_and_link_to_container production_batch_001 sample_vial_001 sample_container_001)
(record_batch_lab_receipt production_batch_001 sample_vial_001 sample_container_001 assay_method_a)
(attach_calibration_certificate_to_batch production_batch_001 calibration_certificate_a)
(assign_technician_to_plan production_batch_001 technician_a)
(prepare_batch_for_analysis_standard_path production_batch_001 calibration_certificate_a technician_a sample_vial_001 sample_container_001)
(attach_regulatory_document_to_batch production_batch_001 regulatory_document_a)
(finalize_analysis_preparation_no_container_flags production_batch_001 regulatory_document_a sample_vial_001 sample_container_001)
(record_batch_release_and_mark_ready production_batch_001)
(finalize_sampling_plan_and_attach_release_document production_batch_001 release_document_a assay_method_a)
(authorize_batch_release production_batch_001 quality_reviewer_3 release_document_a)
(initialize_sampling_plan production_batch_002)
(assign_quality_reviewer_to_plan production_batch_002 quality_reviewer_3)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan production_batch_001 assay_method_a)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan production_batch_002 assay_method_a)
(confirm_assay_method_assignment production_batch_002 assay_method_a)
(vialize_sample_and_link_to_container production_batch_002 sample_vial_002 sample_container_001)
(record_batch_lab_receipt production_batch_002 sample_vial_002 sample_container_001 assay_method_a)
(detach_calibration_certificate_from_batch production_batch_001 calibration_certificate_a)
(attach_calibration_certificate_to_batch production_batch_002 calibration_certificate_a)
(release_technician_from_plan production_batch_001 technician_a)
(assign_technician_to_plan production_batch_002 technician_a)
(prepare_batch_for_analysis_standard_path production_batch_002 calibration_certificate_a technician_a sample_vial_002 sample_container_001)
(detach_regulatory_document_from_batch production_batch_001 regulatory_document_a)
(attach_regulatory_document_to_batch production_batch_002 regulatory_document_a)
(finalize_analysis_preparation_no_container_flags production_batch_002 regulatory_document_a sample_vial_002 sample_container_001)
(record_batch_release_and_mark_ready production_batch_002)
(finalize_sampling_plan_and_attach_release_document production_batch_002 release_document_a assay_method_a)
(authorize_batch_release production_batch_002 quality_reviewer_3 release_document_a)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan production_batch_002 assay_method_a)
(record_production_unit_release production_unit_1 sample_container_001)
(record_qc_unit_release qc_unit_1 sample_container_001)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(finalize_sampling_plan_and_attach_release_document production_unit_1 release_document_a assay_method_a)
(authorize_production_unit_release production_unit_1 quality_reviewer_1 release_document_a)
(unassign_assay_method_from_plan production_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(assign_assay_method_to_plan qc_unit_1 assay_method_a)
(finalize_sampling_plan_and_attach_release_document qc_unit_1 release_document_a assay_method_a)
(authorize_qc_unit_release qc_unit_1 quality_reviewer_2 release_document_a)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for pharmaceutics in-process sampling planning. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Task  
- The problem focuses on creating a plan for in-process sampling in pharmaceutics using PDDL (Planning Domain Definition Language).  
- The output must be an executable plan, formatted as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, with one action per line.  
- Each action follows the structure (action-name arg1 arg2 ...), ensuring clarity and executability.  

## Outlining the Approach  
- The task requires generating a sequence of specific, grounded actions based on the PDDL planning problem.  
- The focus is on pharmaceutics sampling, suggesting actions will relate to sampling processes and resource management.

## Outlining the Approach  
- To achieve the goal, initialize sampling plans for production_unit_1, qc_unit_1, production_batch_001, and production_batch_002, starting with uninitialized plans.  
- Actions include assigning quality reviewers, assay methods, and technicians to each plan, ensuring prerequisites like reviewer availability are met.  

## Exploring Action Dependencies  
- Ready_for_release_flag is needed for finalizing sampling plans, achievable via multiple actions like record_batch_release_and_mark_ready or record_final_qa_release, depending on batch status.  
- Each action has specific preconditions, such as analysis_complete or regulatory_doc_attached_flag, and sets flags like batch_release_recorded or ready_for_release.  

## Analyzing Resource Sharing  
- Multiple plans can share the same assay method (e.g., assay_method_a), with assignment actions marking it unavailable until reassigned or unassigned.  
- Batches 001 and 002 share the same container and production/qc units, suggesting coordinated actions for resource management.  

## Investigating Sampling Processes  
- Sampling collection involves scheduling points, allocating kits, and collecting samples, with actions like assign_technician_to_plan requiring technician availability.  
- Actions like confirm_assay_method_assignment ensure assay methods are finalized, with options to unassign if needed for flexibility.

## Checking Resource Constraints  
- Calibration_a and protocol_a/b are available, along with QC checklist_a/b, suggesting shared resources for both batches.  
- With only 3 reviewers for 4 plans (production_unit_1, qc_unit_1, batch_001, batch_002), assignment may require cycling reviewers, as authorize_release makes them available again.  

## Planning Initial Actions  
- Start by initializing sampling plans for production_unit_1, qc_unit_1, batch_001, and batch_002 to set up for subsequent actions.  
- Assign quality reviewers (e.g., quality_reviewer_1 to production_unit_1), then assign and confirm assay methods like assay_method_a, ensuring prerequisites are met.  

## Examining Sampling Dependencies  
- Sampling actions like allocating kits or collecting samples (e.g., allocate_sampling_kit_to_production_point) require confirmed assay methods, emphasizing the need for prior confirmation.  
- Production and QC units need sampling collection completed and confirmed before recording unit release, which is necessary for the ready_for_release flag.  

## Exploring Release Authorization  
- Units must have container_checked_in and ready_flags set, achievable via record_unit_release after sampling, to reach release_authorized status.  
- Batches likely depend on unit readiness, suggesting a sequence where units are finalized before batch actions, given shared container usage.

## Managing Batch Release

- Attaching regulatory documents (e.g., regulatory_document_a) to batch001 requires confirmation and makes the document unavailable for other uses.
- Finalizing analysis for batch001 without container flags sets analysis_complete, allowing recording of batch release and marking it ready_for_release.

## Handling Resource Limitations

- With only one calibration certificate (calib_a) and regulatory document, resources are constrained for batch002, as attach actions mark them unavailable.
- Detaching calibration or regulatory documents from batch001 is possible, freeing them for use with batch002, ensuring flexibility in resource management.

## Addressing Reviewer Availability

- All three reviewers are currently assigned, blocking progress for batch002, requiring a reviewer to be freed through authorization.
- Authorizing batch001’s release finalizes it, making quality_reviewer_3 available again for reassigning to other plans like batch002.

## Continuing Resource Management  
- Assay methods remain assigned to plans after finalization, requiring explicit unassignment (e.g., unassign_assay_method_from_plan) to free them for other uses.  
- Detaching and reattaching calibration certificates and regulatory documents allows resource reuse, ensuring flexibility for multiple batches like batch001 and batch002.  

## Sequencing Unit Authorizations  
- Production units like production_unit_1 require finalized sampling plans and attached release documents before authorization, freeing reviewers and documents afterward.  
- Quality control units follow a similar process, unassigning assay methods from prior units to reassign, ensuring all units reach release_authorized status.  

## Outlining Action Sequence  
- Initialize sampling plans and assign quality reviewers and assay methods to start, followed by confirming assay methods for production units.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
